verses.commentary: Pursue love as primary; eagerly desire spiritual gifts, especially prophecy. Love governs the exercise of gifts. Prophecy's intelligibility makes it particularly valuable for corporate worship.
Tongues address God, not people. No one understands; mysteries are uttered by the Spirit. Private prayer language, not public communication.
Prophecy speaks to people for strengthening, encouragement, and comfort. Intelligible speech edifies the community. Communication builds up the body.
Tongues edify the speaker; prophecy edifies the church. Personal benefit versus corporate benefit. Worship prioritizes communal edification over individual experience.

Paul wants all to speak in tongues but prefers they prophesy. Prophecy is greater unless tongues are interpreted so the church is edified. Interpretation makes tongues equivalent to prophecy in value.
If Paul spoke in tongues without revelation, knowledge, prophecy, or teaching, what good would it do? Unintelligibility renders speech useless for others.
Musical instruments must produce distinct notes or the melody is unrecognizable. Communication requires clarity. Indistinct sounds convey no meaning.
An unclear trumpet call fails to rally troops for battle. Communication requires precision and intelligibility. Ambiguity produces confusion, not mobilization.
Speech without intelligible words is speaking into the air. Communication demands comprehension. Incomprehensible utterance achieves nothing.
Languages in the world are numerous, yet none is without meaning. Every language communicates to those who understand it. The issue is comprehensibility, not validity.
Without understanding the language, speaker and hearer are foreigners to each other. Mutual incomprehension creates relational distance. Language barrier prevents fellowship.
Since you're eager for spiritual gifts, excel in gifts that build up the church. Ambition should target edification. Spiritual zeal must serve communal good.
Tongue speakers should pray for interpretation ability. Personal gift requires complementary gift for corporate benefit. Completeness comes through combination.
Praying in a tongue engages the spirit but leaves the mind unfruitful. Ecstatic prayer bypasses rational comprehension. Spiritual activity without intellectual engagement.
Pray and sing with both spirit and mind. Integration of spiritual experience and rational understanding. Holistic worship engages the whole person.
If you praise in the spirit, how can outsiders say Amen if they don't understand? Participation requires comprehension. Unintelligibility excludes others from corporate worship.
Your thanksgiving may be excellent, but no one else is edified. Personal enrichment divorced from communal benefit violates worship's corporate purpose.
Paul thanks God he speaks in tongues more than all of them. His critique isn't from inexperience or jealousy but from practiced wisdom. He values the gift privately.
In church, five intelligible words for instruction surpass ten thousand words in a tongue. Clarity and edification trump volume and experience. Corporate context demands comprehension.
Stop thinking like children. Be infants regarding evil but adults in thinking. Maturity discerns what builds up versus what merely impresses.
Isaiah prophesied God would speak through foreign tongues to Israel, yet they wouldn't listen. Tongues function as judgment sign to unbelieving Israel.
Tongues are a sign for unbelievers, not believers. Prophecy is for believers, not unbelievers. Function differs based on audience and purpose.
If the whole church speaks in tongues when unbelievers enter, they'll think you're insane. Incomprehensibility to outsiders creates scandal, not witness.
If all prophesy and an unbeliever enters, they're convicted by all, examined by all. Intelligible proclamation exposes heart secrets, revealing God's presence.
Heart secrets laid bare provoke worship: "God is really among you!" Prophecy penetrates, convicts, and converts. Intelligibility produces authentic encounter with God.
When you gather, each brings something: hymn, teaching, revelation, tongue, interpretation. Everything must build up. Diversity serves edification, not performance.
If anyone speaks in a tongue, two or at most three should speak, one at a time, with interpretation. Order and intelligibility govern corporate use.
Without an interpreter, tongue speakers should remain silent in church and speak to themselves and God. Public worship requires comprehension.
Two or three prophets should speak; others should weigh what's said. Prophecy requires discernment. Evaluation protects against error.
If revelation comes to someone seated, the first speaker should stop. Spontaneity and order coexist. Flexibility serves the Spirit's movement.
All can prophesy in turn so everyone may be instructed and encouraged. Orderly succession maximizes edification. Turn-taking serves learning.
Prophets' spirits are subject to prophets' control. The Spirit doesn't override self-control. Charismatic manifestation doesn't excuse chaos.
God is not a God of disorder but of peace, as in all congregations. Divine character defines worship culture. Order reflects God's nature.
Women should remain silent in churches, not permitted to speak, but must be in submission. Controversial instruction likely addresses disruptive questioning or authoritative teaching, not all speech.

If they want to inquire, ask husbands at home. It's disgraceful for a woman to speak in church. Cultural context shapes application. The principle addresses disruptive behavior, not universal silencing.
Did God's word originate with you? Are you the only ones it reached? Rhetorical rebuke challenges Corinthian exceptionalism. They must conform to universal church practice.
If anyone thinks they're a prophet or spiritual, let them acknowledge that Paul's instructions are the Lord's command. Apostolic authority carries divine weight.
If anyone ignores this, they will be ignored. Rejection of apostolic instruction results in exclusion from recognition. Authority demands submission.
Be eager to prophesy; don't forbid speaking in tongues. Balance: prioritize prophecy while allowing tongues. Both gifts have legitimate place.
Everything should be done in fitting and orderly way. Decency and order govern all worship. Freedom operates within structure, not chaos.
